items.headline: Kyle Lowry Retires as a Raptor, Jersey #7 to be Retired
items.summary: NBA Champion Kyle Lowry signed a one-day contract with the Toronto Raptors on Tuesday, July 7, 2026, to officially retire with the franchise where he spent nine seasons and won a title. The Raptors announced plans to retire his No. 7 jersey.

items.headline: Donovan Mitchell Inks Massive $273M Extension with Cavaliers
items.summary: Cleveland Cavaliers star Donovan Mitchell has agreed to a four-year, $273 million contract extension with the team. The seven-time All-Star's deal was finalized on Tuesday, July 8, 2026, solidifying his future in Cleveland.

items.headline: Knicks' Finals MVP Jalen Brunson Undergoes Wrist Surgery
items.summary: New York Knicks' NBA Finals MVP, Jalen Brunson, underwent left wrist surgery and is expected to be fully recovered for the start of the upcoming season. Brunson led the Knicks to their first championship since 1973.

sections.title: Last Night's Games (Salt Lake City Summer League - July 7)

items.headline: Hawks Mount Comeback Against Thunder
items.summary: The Atlanta Hawks defeated the Oklahoma City Thunder 82-77 in a Salt Lake City Summer League matchup, overcoming a 20-point early deficit.

items.headline: Jazz Continue Undefeated Summer League Run
items.summary: The Utah Jazz maintained their perfect record in the Salt Lake City Summer League by defeating the Memphis Grizzlies.

items.headline: Sandfort Shines with 25 Points
items.summary: OKC's Payton Sandfort dropped 25 points, including four three-pointers, in the Thunder's Salt Lake City Summer League loss to the Hawks.

items.headline: Ejiofor's Double-Double Dominance
items.summary: Atlanta's Zuby Ejiofor posted a strong performance with 19 points and 15 rebounds in their victory over the Thunder.

items.headline: Boozer Impresses for Grizzlies
items.summary: No. 3 overall pick Cameron Boozer recorded 18 points, 7 rebounds, and 4 assists for the Memphis Grizzlies in Summer League action.

items.headline: Gillespie's All-Around Game
items.summary: Ja'Kobi Gillespie delivered 19 points (5-9 3PT), 6 assists, and 2 steals in the Spurs' California Classic win over the Lakers on July 6.

items.headline: Acuff's Scoring & Playmaking
items.summary: Sacramento's Darius Acuff Jr. contributed 22 points (7-19 FG, 4-9 3PT), 3 rebounds, 3 assists, 2 blocks, and 1 steal in a California Classic win against the Bucks on July 6.

items.headline: Deni Avdija Reflects on All-Star Season
items.summary: Portland Trail Blazers forward Deni Avdija, a first-time All-Star last season, continues to be a focal point for the franchise as they look to build around his improved play.

items.headline: LeBron James Free Agency Update: Cavs and Sixers Emerge as Top Contenders
items.summary: The race for LeBron James's signature is heating up, with the Cleveland Cavaliers and Philadelphia 76ers reportedly becoming leading contenders. His agent indicated a decision isn't imminent, and it's confirmed James will not be returning to the Lakers.

items.headline: Lakers Pursue Jonathan Kuminga After Hawks Decline Option
items.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers are reportedly targeting Jonathan Kuminga in free agency, offering a two-year, $20 million deal. This comes after the Atlanta Hawks declined Kuminga's $24.3 million player option.

items.headline: Las Vegas Summer League Kicks Off July 9th
items.summary: The highly anticipated Las Vegas NBA Summer League officially begins on Thursday, July 9, running until July 19. Opening night features the top two draft picks, AJ Dybantsa (WAS) vs. Darryn Peterson (UTA).

items.headline: Key Free Agent Signings Bolster Rosters
items.summary: Several notable free agent signings have become official, including Trae Young joining the WAS Wizards on a four-year deal and Kevon Looney heading to the LAL Lakers for one year.
